Imagine being trapped in darkness and silence, unable to tell anyone what you need or how you feel. At six years old, Helen Keller lived in this world. She could feel, smell, and touch everything around her, but she had no words. Every day, her frustration grew until she broke down in tears, kicking and screaming, unable to make herself understood. But then, one day in March 1887, a teacher arrived who would change everything. This is the story of how Helen learned that everything has a name—and how one word, "water," opened a door to a world she never knew existed.
